sample_valid_maps.action: Implement periodic KYC record updation: every 2 years for high-risk, 8 years for medium-risk, and 10 years for low-risk customers
sample_valid_maps.validation_notes: Section 3(b) mandates periodic KYC update by risk category — Compliance owns CDD obligations, Critical due to core regulatory requirement

sample_valid_maps.action: Designate a Principal Officer responsible for filing Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs) with FIU-IND within 7 working days of suspicion formation
sample_valid_maps.validation_notes: Section 6(a-b) mandates STR filing and Principal Officer designation — Compliance owns AML reporting, Critical due to legal obligation under PMLA

sample_valid_maps.action: Implement Board-approved customer risk categorisation policy defining criteria for Low, Medium, and High risk classifications
sample_valid_maps.validation_notes: Section 4(a) mandates risk categorisation policy — Risk owns risk framework design, High priority due to governance requirement

sample_valid_maps.action: Conduct mandatory AML/CFT training for all frontline staff at least once per calendar year; complete induction training for new employees within 30 days of joining
sample_valid_maps.validation_notes: Section 8(a-b) mandates annual AML/CFT training — Compliance owns training obligations, High priority due to recurring regulatory requirement

sample_valid_maps.action: Submit quarterly compliance report on KYC/AML/CFT to RBI within 21 days of quarter-end, certified by Chief Compliance Officer
sample_valid_maps.validation_notes: Section 10(a-b) mandates quarterly RBI reporting — Compliance owns regulatory submissions, High priority due to recurring deadline obligation

sample_valid_maps.action: Implement centralised KYC records management system with cross-branch, cross-product access and 10-year record retention capability
sample_valid_maps.validation_notes: Section 9(b-c) mandates centralised KYC system — IT owns system implementation, Medium priority due to technology infrastructure requirement
